description: Script.fromASM() must treat bare hex strings that look like opcodes as data pushes when they appear in an ASM context that expects data, not as opcode names. ts-sdk pre-fix treated '76' (the hex encoding of OP_DUP) as OP_DUP (opcode 0x76) rather than as a data push of the byte 0x76, producing 'OP_PUSHDATA1' when toHex() was called instead of '0176'.

regression.symptom: Script.fromASM('76').toHex() returned 'OP_PUSHDATA1' (the opcode name, not hex) because '76' was parsed as opcode 0x76 (OP_DUP) then re-serialised
regression.root_cause: fromASM tokeniser resolved bare hex strings against the opcode name table before checking if they were intended as data; '76' matched OP_DUP (0x76)

vectors.description: fromASM('76') must encode as data push of byte 0x76, producing hex '0176'
vectors.notes: 0x76 is the opcode byte for OP_DUP, but in an ASM literal context '76' is a 1-byte data push. The encoded form is OP_PUSHDATA_1byte (0x01) followed by the byte (0x76) = 0176.

vectors.description: fromASM('OP_DUP') must encode as OP_DUP opcode 0x76 (not a data push)
vectors.notes: Control vector: the opcode name 'OP_DUP' must still produce the single opcode byte 0x76, not a data push.

vectors.description: fromASM('OP_DUP OP_HASH160') round-trips correctly to hex
vectors.notes: Regression guard: named opcodes must remain unaffected by the data-push fix.
